tasks.description: Reactivate Manus CMO — re-enable wallet, start PM2 processes, smoke test
tasks.context:
The CMO was deliberately deactivated on 2026-03-13 (CTO-20260222-002). Owner has confirmed reactivation.

PREREQUISITE (human action — do NOT proceed if this is missing):
- Check server .env: grep MANUS_API_KEY /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/.env
- If MANUS_API_KEY is empty or missing: STOP. Report back: 'CMO reactivation blocked — MANUS_API_KEY not set in server .env. Owner must add it.'
- If MANUS_API_KEY is present: proceed with steps below.

STEP 1 — Re-enable CMO wallet in Supabase:
Run on server (uses DATABASE_URL from .env):
  cd /home/invoica/apps/Invoica
  node -e "
    const { PrismaClient } = require('@prisma/client');
    const prisma = new PrismaClient();
    prisma.agentWallet.updateMany({ where: { agentId: 'cmo' }, data: { isActive: true } })
      .then(r => { console.log('updated:', r.count, 'rows'); prisma.$disconnect(); })
      .catch(e => { console.error(e); prisma.$disconnect(); });
  "
If the table/model name is different (e.g. agent_wallets, wallets), check prisma schema first:
  grep -n 'wallet\|agentId\|agent_id' /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/backend/prisma/schema.prisma
If no wallet table exists at all: skip this step and note it in the report.

STEP 2 — Clear stale cron guards:
  rm -f /home/invoica/apps/Invoica/logs/cron-guard-cmo-daily-watch.json

STEP 3 — Start CMO PM2 processes:
  cd /home/invoica/apps/Invoica
  pm2 start ecosystem.config.js --only cmo-daily-watch,cmo-weekly-content-plan
  pm2 save
  pm2 status

STEP 4 — Smoke test (trigger market-watch immediately):
  pm2 restart cmo-daily-watch
  sleep 10 && pm2 logs cmo-daily-watch --lines 30 --nostream
  Check for: Manus task created, polling, completed — OR any API error

STEP 5 — Report results to:
  reports/cto/cmo-reactivation-2026-03-16.md
  Include: MANUS_API_KEY present (yes/no), wallet update result, PM2 status, smoke test output, any errors.

tasks.description: BUG-001: Fix rotate and delete API key routes — wrong service being called
BUG-003: Make API key creation persist to Supabase — fixes ledger verification and post-restart auth
BUG-002: Generate SQL to delete corrupted invoice rows (test data with Unix timestamp as invoiceNumber)
tasks.context:
A Unix timestamp (1772554801) was inserted as an invoiceNumber in the Invoice table (confirmed to be test data, not real user invoices). Since the system generates new invoice numbers via MAX(invoiceNumber) + 1, all subsequent invoices get numbers like 1772554802, 1772554803, etc.

CREATE: scripts/fix-invoice-sequence.sql

The file should contain exactly this SQL (copy verbatim):

-- Invoica Invoice Sequence Fix — Delete test data with corrupted invoiceNumber
-- Run in Supabase Dashboard → SQL Editor → New Query
-- Confirmed: rows with invoiceNumber > 1_000_000_000 are test data, safe to delete

BEGIN;

-- Preview what will be deleted (run this first, verify before committing)
SELECT id, "invoiceNumber", status, "customerEmail", "createdAt"
FROM "Invoice"
WHERE "invoiceNumber" > 1000000000
ORDER BY "invoiceNumber" ASC;

-- Delete corrupted test rows
DELETE FROM "Invoice"
WHERE "invoiceNumber" > 1000000000;

-- Verify: all remaining invoices should have small sequential numbers
SELECT
  COUNT(*) AS remaining_invoices,
  MAX("invoiceNumber") AS highest_invoice_num
FROM "Invoice";

COMMIT;

Also create scripts/fix-invoice-sequence.md:

# Invoice Sequence Fix

1. Open Supabase Dashboard → SQL Editor → New Query
2. Paste scripts/fix-invoice-sequence.sql
3. Run the SELECT first — confirm the rows shown are test data
4. Then run DELETE + COMMIT
5. After this, new invoices will resume from a sane sequential number

Max 25 lines each file.

tasks.description: BUG-005: Webhooks — wire Prisma persistence into routes (replace stub with real storage)
BUG-004: Fix auth error message — mentions Bearer only, but X-API-Key is the documented header
tasks.context:
The requireApiKey middleware in middleware/apiKeyAuth.ts returns an error message that says 'Use Authorization: Bearer sk_<key>' but the API accepts BOTH headers. The api-contract.md documents X-API-Key as the primary header. The error message should mention both.

EDIT: backend/src/middleware/apiKeyAuth.ts

Find this exact line (around line 46):
  message: 'Missing or invalid API key. Use Authorization: Bearer sk_<key>',

Replace with:
  message: 'Missing or invalid API key. Use X-API-Key: sk_<key> header (or Authorization: Bearer sk_<key>)',

That is the ONLY change. One line. Do not touch anything else in this file.

tasks.description: Add Solana invoice creation support — chain param + base58 address validation
Split api-keys page (545 lines) into composable sub-components — CTO-20260215-006
tasks.context:
FILE: frontend/app/dashboard/api-keys/page.tsx (545 lines — violates 200-line rule)

The page contains 3 distinct units that must be extracted into separate files:

1. EXTRACT KeyRevealModal (lines 33–132) → new file: frontend/components/dashboard/api-key-reveal-modal.tsx
   - Props: { keyValue: string; keyName: string; onClose: () => void }
   - Self-contained: manages its own copied state and clipboard logic
   - Export as: export function KeyRevealModal(...)

2. EXTRACT CreateKeyModal (lines 133–251) → new file: frontend/components/dashboard/api-key-create-modal.tsx
   - Props: { onCreated: (key: { value: string; name: string }) => void; onClose: () => void }
   - Contains its own fetch to POST /v1/api-keys — keep it there
   - Export as: export function CreateKeyModal(...)

3. KEEP in page.tsx: ApiKeysPage (lines 252–545) — the main page component
   - Remove the two extracted functions from page.tsx
   - Add imports: import { KeyRevealModal } from '@/components/dashboard/api-key-reveal-modal'; import { CreateKeyModal } from '@/components/dashboard/api-key-create-modal';
   - Keep: formatDate, ApiKeyRecord interface, BACKEND_URL, all page state and fetch logic
   - Result: page.tsx should be ~200 lines after extraction

Rules:
- Do NOT change any logic, props, or behaviour — pure extraction only
- Do NOT rename any functions, variables, or CSS classes
- Each new file must start with 'use client';
- Shared type ApiKeyRecord must be defined in page.tsx and imported by sub-components if needed (or duplicated — keep it simple)
- formatDate stays in page.tsx (only used by page)
- After extraction: npx tsc --noEmit must pass with 0 new errors

output.note: Split via CTO-006 sprint — files placed in app/dashboard/api-keys/ (co-located)

tasks.description: Upgrade OpenClaw v2026.2.26 → v2026.3.13 on Hetzner server
tasks.context:
OpenClaw v2026.3.13 is available. Current install: v2026.2.26 on server at /opt/oc/.

Steps to execute on Hetzner (65.108.90.178, user invoica):

1. Backup current install:
   cp -r /opt/oc /opt/oc.bak-2026.2.26
   cp /home/invoica/.openclaw/openclaw.json /home/invoica/.openclaw/openclaw.json.bak

2. Check changelog before upgrading:
   npx clawhub@latest changelog 2026.3.13
   or: cat /opt/oc/CHANGELOG.md if it exists
   Flag any breaking changes in your report.

3. Upgrade:
   npx clawhub@latest upgrade

4. Verify install version:
   cat /opt/oc/package.json | grep version
   Should read 2026.3.13

5. Restart openclaw-gateway via PM2:
   pm2 restart openclaw-gateway && pm2 status

6. Smoke test — run one trivial sprint task to confirm gateway responds:
   pm2 logs openclaw-gateway --lines 20
   Look for: gateway connected, agents loaded, no errors

7. Run TypeScript check on local codebase to confirm 0 new errors:
   npx tsc --noEmit 2>&1 | grep -v node_modules

8. If any step fails: pm2 stop openclaw-gateway && cp -r /opt/oc.bak-2026.2.26 /opt/oc && pm2 start openclaw-gateway

Report: version before/after, changelog breaking changes, smoke test result, PM2 status.

output.note: Already v2026.3.13. Crash loop was port 18789 race condition. Fixed by stop-all + clean restart. Gateway now stable.

tasks.description: Low-score pattern monitoring — CTO-20260219-003: assess and implement or piggyback
tasks.context:
Score data location: reports/swarm-runs/daily-YYYY-MM-DD.json and reports/swarm-runs/2026*.json
Score field path: run.tasks[].review.score (integer 0–100, field is null when task was rejected without review)

Assessment questions:
1. Scan last 14 days of swarm-runs JSON files. For each agent, calculate:
   - Average score across all reviewed tasks
   - Count of tasks scoring below 70
   - Count of consecutive tasks scoring below 70
2. Define 'low-score pattern': agent average < 75 over last 10 tasks OR 3+ consecutive tasks < 70
3. Decision: is this a one-time report or ongoing alert?
   - Check if agents/skill-observer exists: if yes, piggyback (add a score-pattern check to its prompt)
   - If no: create a new lightweight script at scripts/check-low-score-patterns.ts

If implementing a script:
- Input: reads all reports/swarm-runs/daily-*.json files
- Output: JSON report to reports/cto/low-score-patterns-YYYY-MM-DD.json with structure:
  { date, agents: [{ agent_id, avg_score, low_score_count, consecutive_low, flag: boolean }] }
- Flag = true if avg_score < 75 OR consecutive_low >= 3
- Script must be runnable: ts-node scripts/check-low-score-patterns.ts

If piggybacking on skill-observer:
- Read agents/skill-observer/prompt.md first
- Add score-pattern analysis instructions to the existing prompt
- Document what was added in your report

Final deliverable: either the script OR the updated prompt, plus a report at reports/cto/low-score-assessment-2026-03-16.md summarising the decision and any patterns found in the last 14 days.

output.note: Script created. Only 4 scored tasks found (swarm inactive). No real patterns to detect yet. Revisit when sprint runner resumes.

tasks.description: Response caching go/no-go — CTO-20260216-003: assess token waste from repeated prompts
tasks.context:
Question: does the orchestrator send duplicate or near-duplicate prompts to agents across sprints, wasting tokens?

Data sources to examine:
1. reports/swarm-runs/daily-*.json — task.tokens_used per task
2. backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts — how prompts are constructed
3. sprints/*.json — do repeated agent+description combos appear across files?

Assessment steps:
1. Read backend/src/services/orchestrator.ts — look for where system prompts or task context are assembled. Are any large blocks repeated verbatim on every call (e.g. full SOUL.md, full constitution.md)?
2. Scan last 7 days of swarm-run reports: sum tokens_used per agent per day. Flag agents consuming >50k tokens/day.
3. Check if any 2 tasks across the last 10 sprint files share identical (agent + description) — sign of accidental duplication.
4. Estimate: what % of tokens are from repeated/static context vs novel task content?

Decision criteria:
- Build cache sprint: if >20% of tokens are from repeated static context OR duplicate task combos found
- Close as low-priority: if token usage is mostly unique task content and no duplicates found

Output: report at reports/cto/caching-assessment-2026-03-16.md with:
- Token usage summary by agent (last 7 days)
- Duplicate task combos found (if any)
- % static vs novel prompt content estimate
- Decision: BUILD / CLOSE with one-sentence rationale

output.note: CLOSED / NO-GO. Anthropic server-side prompt caching active. User prompts 100% unique. Client cache hit rate near-zero.
